title: Today's Tech Brief: Feeble Little Horse leans into digital weirdness on bitknot
summary: A look at Feeble Little Horse's digital experiment on bitknot and a curious case of a marketing demand that didn't quite add up.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and I'm excited to share some fresh tech news with you. Let's dive right in. I've got a couple of stories that caught my eye recently, and I think you'll find them interesting.

First up, indie rock band Feeble Little Horse is pushing the boundaries of digital music with their latest experiment on bitknot. According to The Verge, the band has evolved from an exciting indie rock throwback into something more cutting-edge. I'm intrigued to see how their fans respond to this new direction. It's always cool to see artists experimenting with new formats and platforms.

Next, I came across a curious case where marketing demanded IT add a website feature that was already working. This story from The Register has me chuckling - it seems like a classic case of 'someone didn't do their research'. I wonder how many times this has happened in other companies. It's a funny example of how communication breakdowns can lead to some pretty silly situations.

And finally, I stumbled upon another story that's been making waves in the tech world. While I don't have all the details, it's clear that technology is continuing to evolve and surprise us. From digital music experiments to AI-powered everything, it's an exciting time to be alive.

Feeble Little Horse leans into digital weirdness on bitknot, from theverge.com, is also worth a quick mention. My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
